desc: Berat is a UNESCO-listed Albanian hillside city known for Ottoman-era neighborhoods, a dramatic castle district, and warm inland summers with clear shoulder-season touring conditions.
hls: Climate and daylength patterns are imported from the shared monthly climate CSV for consistency.
Shoulder months generally provide the strongest balance of comfort and crowd pressure.
Peak-season demand and pricing pressure increase around summer holiday windows.
Best experiences usually come from matching activity timing to monthly weather patterns.

todo.name: Old-town orientation walk
todo.desc: Start with a guided or self-guided walk through the main historic districts to set context.

todo.name: Top local museum stop
todo.desc: Reserve at least one half-day for the city’s signature museum or cultural site.

todo.name: Scenic viewpoint session
todo.desc: Schedule a golden-hour viewpoint stop for the most photogenic light.

todo.name: Market and food crawl
todo.desc: Use local market streets for regional dishes and low-key evening exploration.

todo.name: Nature/beach side trip
todo.desc: Keep one day flexible for a nearby coast, canyon, or countryside excursion.

todo.name: Slow final-day neighborhood loop
todo.desc: Wrap up with cafés and residential streets outside the main tourist corridors.

wifi.note: Connectivity is usually solid in central stays; confirm speeds directly with your accommodation for work-critical trips.

prac.fltNote: No commercial airport in Berat; most visitors fly into Tirana (TIA) and continue by road.
prac.lang: Albanian is the primary language; English and Italian are common in tourism businesses.
prac.tz: CET (UTC+1) in winter, CEST (UTC+2) in summer.
prac.visa: Entry rules depend on nationality and can change; verify official visa/passport requirements before booking.
alerts: Summer afternoons are very hot in the old town; plan castle climbs early in the day.
Intercity bus frequencies drop in evenings and Sundays; verify return times before day trips.
Cash is still useful for small guesthouses and local taxis outside peak routes.

sweet: April-June and September-October typically offer the best balance of sightseeing comfort and manageable demand.
